edges.description: Loss of any single step in the FAR1 -> GNPAT -> AGPS sequence blocks ether-phospholipid synthesis and depletes cellular plasmalogens.
Plasmalogen deficiency disrupts cartilage and bone development, producing rhizomelic shortening and chondrodysplasia punctata.
Plasmalogen deficiency disrupts lens fiber development, producing congenital cataracts.
Plasmalogen deficiency disrupts CNS neuronal and myelin development, producing intellectual disability, epilepsy, and (in FAR1 deficiency) microcephaly and spasticity.
Decreased plasmalogens report failure of ether-lipid synthesis at the affected enzyme step.

meta.association: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in AGPS (encoding alkyl-DHAP synthase) cause RCDP type 3, an isolated alkyl-DHAP synthase deficiency. ClinGen classifies the gene-disease relationship as Definitive.

evidence.reference_title: Alkyl-dihydroxyacetonephosphate synthase. Fate in peroxisome biogenesis disorders and identification of the point mutation underlying a single enzyme deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Expression of a recombinant protein carrying this mutation in Escherichia coli yielded an inactive enzyme

nodes.description: Congenital cataracts occur across all three single-enzyme forms and are a prominent feature of FAR1 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: severe intellectual disability, early-onset epilepsy, microcephaly, congenital cataracts, growth retardation, and spasticity.

nodes.description: Surgical removal of congenital cataracts can restore and/or preserve vision.

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: severe intellectual disability, early-onset epilepsy, microcephaly, congenital cataracts, growth retardation, and spasticity.

nodes.description: Plasmalogen deficiency disrupts lens fiber development, producing cataracts usually present at birth or in early infancy; cataract is a consistent feature across all three single-enzyme forms.

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: we report the identification of mutations in another gene in plasmalogen biosynthesis, fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 (FAR1), in two families affected by severe intellectual disability, early-onset epilepsy, microcephaly, congenital cataracts, growth retardation, and spasticity.

meta.association: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in FAR1 cause RCDP type 4 (FAR1 deficiency) by abolishing the rate-limiting fatty acyl-CoA reductase step of plasmalogen synthesis. ClinGen classifies the gene-disease relationship as Moderate.

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: all three mutations abolished the reductase activity of FAR1, given that no fatty alcohols could be detected.

meta.association: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in GNPAT (encoding DHAPAT) cause RCDP type 2, an isolated DHAPAT deficiency. ClinGen classifies the gene-disease relationship as Definitive.

evidence.reference_title: Acyl-CoA:dihydroxyacetonephosphate acyltransferase: cloning of the human cDNA and resolution of the molecular basis in rhizomelic chondrodysplasia punctata type 2.
evidence.snippet: All RCDP type 2 patients analyzed were found to contain mutations in their DHAPAT cDNA. This demonstrates that RCDP type 2 is the result of mutations in DHAPAT.

nodes.description: CNS plasmalogen deficiency impairs neuronal and myelin development, producing severe intellectual disability and seizures; FAR1 deficiency additionally features microcephaly and spasticity.

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: in two families affected by severe intellectual disability, early-onset epilepsy, microcephaly, congenital cataracts, growth retardation, and spasticity.

nodes.description: Plasmalogens are ether glycerophospholipids with a vinyl-ether bond at the sn-1 position. Their depletion disrupts membrane and myelin composition and developmental signaling in bone, brain, lens, and other tissues, accounting for the skeletal, ocular, and neurologic manifestations shared with RCDP1.

evidence.reference_title: Functions of plasmalogen lipids in health and disease.
evidence.snippet: Their biosynthesis starts in peroxisomes, and defects at these steps cause the malformation syndrome, Rhizomelic Chondrodysplasia Punctata (RCDP).

nodes.description: Loss-of-function of a single ether-lipid biosynthetic enzyme — FAR1 (rate-limiting fatty-alcohol supply), GNPAT/DHAPAT (first acyltransferase step), or AGPS/alkyl-DHAP synthase (ether-bond formation) — blocks plasmalogen synthesis at a discrete step. Unlike RCDP1, the peroxisomal PTS2 import machinery is intact, so only the affected enzyme is lost and other PTS2-dependent functions (notably phytanic acid alpha-oxidation by phytanoyl-CoA hydroxylase) are preserved.

evidence.reference_title: Acyl-CoA:dihydroxyacetonephosphate acyltransferase: cloning of the human cDNA and resolution of the molecular basis in rhizomelic chondrodysplasia punctata type 2.
evidence.snippet: Patients in the two other subgroups have been reported to be either deficient in the activity of DHAPAT (RCDP type 2) or alkyl-DHAP synthase (RCDP type 3) while no other abnormalities could be observed.

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: all three mutations abolished the reductase activity of FAR1, given that no fatty alcohols could be detected.

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: We also observed reduced plasmalogens in red blood cells in one individual to a range similar to that seen in individuals with RCDP, further supporting abolished FAR1 activity.

readouts.interpretation: Decreased plasmalogens report failure of ether-lipid synthesis at the affected enzyme step.

nodes.description: Seizures/early-onset epilepsy occur in the RCDP group and are prominent in FAR1 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: severe intellectual disability, early-onset epilepsy, microcephaly

nodes.description: Plasmalogen-dependent disruption of cartilage and bone development produces rhizomelic (proximal) limb shortening, chondrodysplasia punctata with stippled epiphyses, and coronal vertebral clefts in RCDP2/3 (and variably in RCDP4).

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Rhizomelic chondrodysplasia punctata (RCDP) is a group of disorders with overlapping clinical features including rhizomelia, chondrodysplasia punctata, coronal clefts, cervical dysplasia, congenital cataracts, profound postnatal growth retardation, severe intellectual disability, and seizures.

nodes.description: Spasticity is a distinguishing feature of FAR1 deficiency (RCDP4).

evidence.reference_title: A peroxisomal disorder of severe intellectual disability, epilepsy, and cataracts due to fatty acyl-CoA reductase 1 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: congenital cataracts, growth retardation, and spasticity.

nodes.description: Management is supportive: physical therapy and orthopedic procedures for contractures and skeletal deformity, antiseizure medication for epilepsy, and developmental support.

evidence.reference_title: Functions of plasmalogen lipids in health and disease.
evidence.snippet: Their biosynthesis starts in peroxisomes, and defects at these steps cause the malformation syndrome, Rhizomelic Chondrodysplasia Punctata (RCDP).
